original_abstract:
This paper introduces **CodeT5+**, a new family of encoder-decoder open large language models (LLMs) designed to overcome the architectural rigidities and objective-specific limitations of existing code models. While many models are optimized for either understanding or generation, CodeT5+ utilizes a flexible modular design that allows it to operate in encoder-only, decoder-only, or encoder-decoder modes depending on the downstream task. This versatility is supported by a diverse mixture of pretraining objectives—including span denoising, causal language modeling, contrastive learning, and text-code matching—applied to both unimodal code and bimodal text-code datasets. 

To achieve scalability, the authors propose a compute-efficient training strategy that initializes the model with frozen, off-the-shelf LLMs, and they further explore instruction tuning to improve natural language alignment. Evaluated across more than 20 code intelligence benchmarks, CodeT5+ achieves state-of-the-art results in various settings, including zero-shot and retrieval-augmented generation. Notably, the instruction-tuned 16B version sets a new performance benchmark for open-source models on the HumanEval task, achieving 35.0% pass@1 and surpassing OpenAI’s code-cushman-001. All models and code are made publicly available to support the research community.
humanized_abstract:
We present CodeT5+, a new family of encoder-decoder open large language models (LLMs) designed to address the limitations of current code models that are typically trained for either the encoding or the decoding tasks only. Our model is trained in a flexible way, that can be used in the form of an encoder only, a decoder only or an encoder-decoder, which is not common for most current models. CodeT5+ is trained with a rich mixture of pretraining objectives, i.e., span denoising, causal language modeling, contrastive learning and text-code matching on unimodal code and bimodal text-code datasets.

Our work seeks to scale large language models for code intelligence and proposes a compute-efficient training methodology that leverages off-the-shelf frozen pre-trained large language models (LLMs) and also focuses on instruction tuning for natural language alignment. Using over 20 code intelligence benchmarks, we demonstrate that CodeT5+ outperforms the state of the art in a diverse set of settings including zero-shot and retrieval-augmented generation. In particular, our instruction-tuned 16B model becomes the new open-source performance benchmark for the HumanEval task, achieving 35.0% pass@1 and surpassing the state-of-the-art from OpenAI’s code-cushman-001. Our models and code are open for the community to utilize.

Our work seeks to scale large language models for code intelligence and proposes a compute-efficient training methodology that leverages off-the-shelf frozen pre-trained large language models (LLMs) and also focuses on instruction tuning for natural language alignment.
